subtasks.description: Retrieve project requirements and backlog items from Jira using the Jira API
Query team member availability from calendar API to identify common free slots
Schedule standup meeting via calendar API and share agenda with team through Slack channel

plan.cost_rationale: This multi-tool orchestration requires 3 subtasks: (1) Jira retrieval (independent), (2) Calendar availability check (independent), then (3) Schedule + Slack notification as a combined downstream action that depends on the first two. All subtasks use multitool_plan since they require invoking specific tools from the enterprise registry (Jira, Calendar, Slack). Tiers: small for simple Jira read and calendar query; mid for the final scheduling step which must synthesize availability data, create a meeting, and coordinate the Slack notification in one coherent action. No cross_recon needed (single source per tool, no reconciliation). No policy_action needed (no governance/compliance evaluation requested).

arguments.title: Cross-Team Standup - Project Requirements Review
arguments.description: Review project requirements and backlog items from Jira. Tickets retrieved: $1.count

arguments.message:
📋 **Project Requirements & Backlog Review**

**Meeting Scheduled:** $4.event_id
**Requirements Retrieved:** $1.count tickets
**Backlog Items:** $1.results

**Agenda:**
• Review open requirements
• Prioritize backlog items
• Identify dependencies
• Assign ownership

Team availability snapshot from calendar: $2.available_slots

📅 Cross-Team Standup Scheduled

**Project Requirements:** $1.results

**Meeting Details:**
Time: 2024-01-04 10:00 AM
Duration: 30 minutes

**Agenda:**
1. Review project requirements
2. Identify blockers
3. Assign action items

Calendar invite sent to attendees.
